description: OP-CAP-11I scripted demo scenario pack. Deterministic, fake, non-production. Each entry is a scripted story keyed to an OP-CAP-11H demo scenario code and references the realistic-but-fake seed dataset (tenant-demo.json, customers-demo.json, products-demo.json, inventory-movements-demo.json, reconciliation-demo.json, telegram-rfq-demo.json, pdf-po-exception-demo.json, bad-ai-output-demo.json, pilot-shadow-demo.json). This file carries no credentials, no real customers, and no executable instructions; all input samples are data only.

scenarios.title: Telegram RFQ with substitution
scenarios.inputSample: Need 2 EA PAD-OE-04465 brake pads for Toyota Camry 2018, wholesale, Almaty.
scenarios.expectedInterpretation: Resolve customer alias 'brake pads for Toyota Camry 2018' to SKU PAD-OE-04465; PAD-OE-04465 is out of stock (snapshot DEMO-STAGE11C-ORIGINAL-UNAVAILABLE, available 0); compatible substitute PAD-SUB-ADV is in stock and accepted for this customer.
scenarios.expectedValidationResult: Draft/RFQ review case created with a suggested compatible substitute; PAD-SUB-ECON remains a blocked substitute for this customer.
scenarios.expectedExceptionOrApproval: Human reviews and confirms the substitute; the bot never approves a quote or order.
scenarios.safetyBoundary: Advisory only. Substitute is a suggestion; no outbound production Telegram send; no business write by the bot.
scenarios.evidenceImpact: Maps to pilot-shadow-demo.json 'Out-of-stock item compatible substitute reviewed' (predictionType SUBSTITUTION, exceptionCategory OUT_OF_STOCK_SUBSTITUTE), moving TELEGRAM_RFQ_SUBSTITUTION from PARTIAL to READY_FOR_SCRIPTED_DEMO.
scenarios.knownLimitation: Telegram intake is local/dev and mock-extraction only; no production outbound messaging.

scenarios.title: PDF purchase order with validation/exception handling
scenarios.inputSample: Plain-text purchase-order source standing in for a PDF upload (no binary PDF is loaded): 4 line items, one with an ambiguous SKU and one with an unsupported unit of measure.
scenarios.expectedInterpretation: Extract 4 lines; PAD-OE-04465 resolves cleanly; 'CAMRY PADS' is an ambiguous alias; 'BX' is an unrecognized unit of measure.
scenarios.expectedValidationResult: Deterministic validation raises exceptions on the ambiguous SKU line and the unit-of-measure line and routes them to operator review.
scenarios.expectedExceptionOrApproval: Operator resolves the ambiguous SKU and UOM before the draft can advance; no auto-approval.
scenarios.safetyBoundary: AI extraction is advisory; deterministic validation is the controlled gate; no ERP/1C/connector write.
scenarios.evidenceImpact: Maps to pilot-shadow-demo.json 'PDF purchase-order extraction with low-confidence correction' (predictionType EXTRACTION, exceptionCategory LOW_CONFIDENCE_EXTRACTION), moving PDF_PO_EXCEPTION from PARTIAL to READY_FOR_SCRIPTED_DEMO.
scenarios.knownLimitation: Production OCR/LLM extraction is not enabled; the source fixture is plain text, not a binary PDF.

scenarios.title: Discount/margin guardrail requiring approval
scenarios.inputSample: Operator requests a 35% discount on PAD-SUB-ADV (cost 25.00) that drops the line margin below the demo margin threshold.
scenarios.expectedInterpretation: Discount lowers margin under the configured threshold for this tenant.
scenarios.expectedValidationResult: Guardrail flags a margin breach; the line is marked as requiring manager approval.
scenarios.expectedExceptionOrApproval: Explicit manager approval is required before the discount can proceed; no auto-approval.
scenarios.safetyBoundary: Risky margin/discount changes never auto-approve; the decision is deterministic and audit-oriented.
scenarios.evidenceImpact: Maps to pilot-shadow-demo.json 'Discount/margin violation requiring manager approval' (predictionType VALIDATION, exceptionCategory MARGIN_VIOLATION), moving DISCOUNT_MARGIN_GUARDRAIL from PARTIAL to READY_FOR_SCRIPTED_DEMO.
scenarios.knownLimitation: The discount request is a scripted internal action; the concrete threshold value is tenant configuration, not a fixture constant.

scenarios.title: Inventory mismatch / reconciliation discrepancy
scenarios.inputSample: PAD-OE-04465 at WH-ALM: opening 150, sale 34, expected 116, actual count 100 (mismatch -16).
scenarios.expectedInterpretation: Expected stock (116) differs from the actual count (100) by -16 units.
scenarios.expectedValidationResult: A reconciliation discrepancy case opens with HIGH severity for review.
scenarios.expectedExceptionOrApproval: Reconciliation surfaces the discrepancy for human review; it performs no ERP write-back.
scenarios.safetyBoundary: Severity and case creation are deterministic and audited; no external write.
scenarios.evidenceImpact: Reconciliation runs are not yet composed into the pilot evidence report; this scenario stays honestly PARTIAL and is demonstrated live via the /reconciliation workspace.
scenarios.knownLimitation: Reconciliation outcomes are not yet recorded as pilot shadow evidence, so readiness does not advance from seeded evidence alone.

scenarios.title: Bad AI output / unsafe input rejection
scenarios.inputSample: Prompt-injection-like customer text plus a structurally malformed model output (both are TEST DATA only).
scenarios.expectedInterpretation: Customer/document text is treated as hostile input; the override attempt is ignored; the malformed extraction fails schema validation.
scenarios.expectedValidationResult: The unsafe output is rejected/sanitized and kept as untrusted data; nothing is interpreted as a system instruction.
scenarios.expectedExceptionOrApproval: No business write occurs; the input is quarantined for review, not executed.
scenarios.safetyBoundary: AI output is advisory and must pass deterministic validation; injected text never becomes code, SQL, a template, or an executable behavior.
scenarios.evidenceImpact: Code-level safety demonstration backed by ExtractionSchemaValidator, PromptInjectionGuardService, AiOutputSanitizer and the pilot DTO safety tests; READY_FOR_SCRIPTED_DEMO with no seeded data needed.
scenarios.knownLimitation: This is a deterministic safety demonstration; it does not invoke a real AI provider.
